I was dressed down in front of everyone and I just want to vanish.
My project failed publicly and I cannot meet anyone's eyes.
They passed me over again and I feel utterly worthless.
Everyone laughed at me and I shrank into the corner.
I said the wrong thing and the whole room went quiet; I am mortified.
I was not invited and I feel small and forgotten.
I slipped in the mud in front of everyone and just lay there defeated.
The gray drizzle matches how low and beaten I feel.
I trudge home in the rain, head down, wanting to disappear.
I sit alone with my cold plate, too ashamed to look up.
I knocked over the dish in front of everyone and could have died.
I have no appetite; the embarrassment has hollowed me out.
I lie in bed replaying the humiliation, sick with shame.
I pull the covers over my head, defeated by the whole day.
I cannot rest; I just keep reliving how small I felt.
I came in dead last and could not look anyone in the eye.
I fumbled the play and let the whole team down; I am crushed.
I gave up halfway, ashamed of how weak I am.
I got lost and had to be rescued, mortified the whole way back.
My bag burst open in the terminal and I scrambled, red-faced.
I missed the train through my own fault and just sank onto the bench.
My side lost badly and I feel beaten down by every headline.
I see my mistake reported and want to crawl into a hole.
The story shamed me publicly and I have no reply.
I broke it again and stand there, useless and small.
The mess is mine and I feel too defeated to even start.
I sit in the dark of my own home, hollow and ashamed.
